Let's integrate multi-blender version support. Each blender version can be called differently. Let's support 4.5 LTS, 5.0, and 4.2 LTS on the same codebase.

```json
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the Atomic preferences UI, preferences properties, and\r\nsome functions for syncing the preference properties with external factors.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\n# updater removed in Blender 4.5 extension format\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _get_addon_prefs():\r\n    # robustly find our AddonPreferences instance regardless of module name\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"bl_idname\") and ap.bl_idname == ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel.bl_idname:\r\n            return ap\r\n        # fallback: match by known property\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the Atomic preferences UI, preferences properties, and\r\nsome functions for syncing the preference properties with external factors.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\n# updater removed in Blender 4.5 extension format\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _get_addon_prefs():\r\n    # robustly find our AddonPreferences instance regardless of module name\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"bl_idname\") and ap.bl_idname == ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel.bl_idname:\r\n            return ap\r\n        # fallback: match by known property\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 53

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains operations for missing file handling. This includes\r\nthe option to reload, remove, replace, and search for these missing files.\r\n\r\nIt also contains the post-reload report dialog that appears after\r\nattempting to reload missing project files.\r\n\r\n# TODO: implement missing file replace and search once Blender fixes the\r\n# TODO: bugs with the file chooser not opening from a dialog\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom ..stats import missing\r\nfrom ..ui.utils import ui_layouts\r\n\r\n\r\n# Atomic Data Manager Reload Missing Files Operator\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_reload_missing(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Reload missing files\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.reload_missing\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Reload Missing Files\"\r\n\r\n    def execute(self, context):\r\n        # reload images\r\n        for image in bpy.data.images:\r\n            image.reload()\r\n\r\n        # reload libraries\r\n        for library in bpy.data.libraries:\r\n            library.reload()\r\n\r\n        # call reload report\r\n        bpy.ops.atomic.reload_report('INVOKE_DEFAULT')\r\n        return {'FINISHED'}",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains operations for missing file handling. This includes\r\nthe option to reload, remove, replace, and search for these missing files.\r\n\r\nIt also contains the post-reload report dialog that appears after\r\nattempting to reload missing project files.\r\n\r\n# TODO: implement missing file replace and search once Blender fixes the\r\n# TODO: bugs with the file chooser not opening from a dialog\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom ..stats import missing\r\nfrom ..ui.utils import ui_layouts\r\n\r\n\r\n# Atomic Data Manager Reload Missing Files Operator\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_reload_missing(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Reload missing files\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.reload_missing\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Reload Missing Files\"\r\n\r\n    def execute(self, context):\r\n        # reload images\r\n        for image in bpy.data.images:\r\n            image.reload()\r\n\r\n        # reload libraries\r\n        for library in bpy.data.libraries:\r\n            library.reload()\r\n\r\n        # call reload report\r\n        bpy.ops.atomic.reload_report('INVOKE_DEFAULT')\r\n        return {'FINISHED'}",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the direct use operators, intended to be used with\r\nAtomic's pie menu interface. However, they can be implemented anywhere\r\nif they need to be.\r\n\r\nThese operators basically wrap the functions from ops.utils.nuke.py and\r\nops.utils.clean.py into operators so they can be easily called by other\r\nintefaces in Blender.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\nfrom ..stats import unused\r\nfrom .utils import nuke\r\nfrom .utils import clean\r\nfrom ..ui.utils import ui_layouts\r\n\r\n\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_invoke_pie_menu_ui(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Invokes Atomic's pie menu UI if the \\\"Enable Pie Menu UI\\\"\r\n    preference is enabled in Atomic's preferences panel\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.invoke_pie_menu_ui\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Invoke Pie Menu UI\"\r\n\r\n    def execute(self, context):\r\n        if config.enable_pie_menu_ui:\r\n            bpy.ops.wm.call_menu_pie(name=\"ATOMIC_MT_main_pie\")\r\n        return {'FINISHED'}",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the direct use operators, intended to be used with\r\nAtomic's pie menu interface. However, they can be implemented anywhere\r\nif they need to be.\r\n\r\nThese operators basically wrap the functions from ops.utils.nuke.py and\r\nops.utils.clean.py into operators so they can be easily called by other\r\nintefaces in Blender.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\nfrom ..stats import unused\r\nfrom .utils import nuke\r\nfrom .utils import clean\r\nfrom ..ui.utils import ui_layouts\r\n\r\n\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_invoke_pie_menu_ui(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Invokes Atomic's pie menu UI if the \\\"Enable Pie Menu UI\\\"\r\n    preference is enabled in Atomic's preferences panel\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.invoke_pie_menu_ui\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Invoke Pie Menu UI\"\r\n\r\n    def execute(self, context):\r\n        if config.enable_pie_menu_ui:\r\n            bpy.ops.wm.call_menu_pie(name=\"ATOMIC_MT_main_pie\")\r\n        return {'FINISHED'}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions that detect missing files in the Blender\r\nproject.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nimport os\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_missing(data):\r\n    # returns a list of keys of unpacked data-blocks with non-existent\r\n    # filepaths\r\n\r\n    missing = []\r\n\r\n    # list of keys that should not be flagged\r\n    do_not_flag = [\"Render Result\", \"Viewer Node\", \"D-NOISE Export\"]\r\n\r\n    for datablock in data:\r\n\r\n        # the absolute path to our data-block\r\n        abspath = bpy.path.abspath(datablock.filepath)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is not packed and has an invalid filepath\r\n        if not datablock.packed_files and not os.path.isfile(abspath):\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is packed but it does not have a filepath\r\n        elif datablock.packed_files and not abspath:\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n    return missing",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "Copyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,

            "text": "---\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "This file contains functions that detect missing files in the Blender\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "project.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "import bpy\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "import os\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "def get_missing(data):\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    # returns a list of keys of unpacked data-blocks with non-existent\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "    # filepaths\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "    missing = []\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "    # list of keys that should not be flagged\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    do_not_flag = [\"Render Result\", \"Viewer Node\", \"D-NOISE Export\"]\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "    for datablock in data:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "        # the absolute path to our data-block\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "        abspath = bpy.path.abspath(datablock.filepath)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "        # if data-block is not packed and has an invalid filepath\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "        if not datablock.packed_files and not os.path.isfile(abspath):\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "            # append it to the missing data list\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "                missing.append(datablock.name)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "        # if data-block is packed but it does not have a filepath\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "        elif datablock.packed_files and not abspath:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "            # append it to the missing data list\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "                missing.append(datablock.name)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "    return missing\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "def images():\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    # returns a list of keys of images with a non-existent filepath\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions that detect missing files in the Blender\r\nproject.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nimport os\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_missing(data):\r\n    # returns a list of keys of unpacked data-blocks with non-existent\r\n    # filepaths\r\n\r\n    missing = []\r\n\r\n    # list of keys that should not be flagged\r\n    do_not_flag = [\"Render Result\", \"Viewer Node\", \"D-NOISE Export\"]\r\n\r\n    for datablock in data:\r\n\r\n        # the absolute path to our data-block\r\n        abspath = bpy.path.abspath(datablock.filepath)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is not packed and has an invalid filepath\r\n        if not datablock.packed_files and not os.path.isfile(abspath):\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is packed but it does not have a filepath\r\n        elif datablock.packed_files and not abspath:\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n    return missing",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 4,
            "line": 64
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "stats/missing.py"
      },
      "score": 0.2109479308128357
    },

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.         "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.       "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions for duplicating data-blocks from Atomic's\r\ninspection interface.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\n\r\n\r\ndef duplicate_data(data, key):\r\n    # creates a copy of the specified data-block and returns its key\r\n    return data[key].copy().name",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "This file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r"

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions for duplicating data-blocks from Atomic's\r\ninspection interface.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\n\r\n\r\ndef duplicate_data(data, key):\r\n    # creates a copy of the specified data-block and returns its key\r\n    return data[key].copy().name",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains miscellaneous statistics functions.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nimport os\r\n\r\n\r\ndef blend_size():\r\n    # returns the size of the current Blender file as a string\r\n\r\n    filepath = bpy.data.filepath\r\n    size_bytes = os.stat(filepath).st_size if filepath != '' else -1\r\n\r\n    kilobyte = 1024  # bytes\r\n    megabyte = 1048576  # bytes\r\n    gigabyte = 1073741824  # bytes\r\n\r\n    if 0 \u003c= size_bytes \u003c kilobyte:\r\n        size_scaled = \"{:.1f} B\".format(size_bytes)\r\n    elif kilobyte \u003c= size_bytes \u003c megabyte:\r\n        size_scaled = \"{:.1f} KB\".format(size_bytes / kilobyte)\r\n    elif megabyte \u003c= size_bytes \u003c gigabyte:\r\n        size_scaled = \"{:.1f} MB\".format(size_bytes / megabyte)\r\n    elif size_bytes \u003e= gigabyte:\r\n        size_scaled = \"{:.1f} GB\".format(size_bytes / gigabyte)\r\n    else:\r\n        size_scaled = \"No Data!\"\r\n\r\n    return size_scaled",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\nreg_list = [ATOMIC_OT_detect_missing]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    for item in reg_list:\r\n        register_class(item)\r\n\r\n    # run missing file auto-detection after loading a Blender file\r\n    bpy.app.handlers.load_post.append(autodetect_missing_files)",
```

Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Multi-Blender Version Support",
  "overview": "Add support for Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0 in the same codebase by implementing version detection, dual metadata format support (bl_info and blender_manifest.toml), and a framework for version-specific API handling.",
  "plan": "# Multi-Blender Version Support Implementation\n\n## Overview\nImplement multi-version support for Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0 by adding version detection infrastructure and supporting both addon metadata formats.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n### 1. Version Detection Module\nCreate &#96;utils/version.py&#96; to:\n- Detect Blender version using &#96;bpy.app.version&#96;\n- Provide version comparison utilities\n- Define version constants for 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0\n- Export a &#96;get_blender_version()&#96; function that returns a normalized version string\n\n### 2. Dual Metadata Format Support\nUpdate &#96;__init__.py&#96; to:\n- Add &#96;bl_info&#96; dictionary for Blender 4.2 LTS compatibility\n- Keep existing &#96;blender_manifest.toml&#96; for 4.5 LTS and 5.0\n- Use version detection to determine which format to register\n- Ensure both formats contain consistent metadata\n\n### 3. Version-Specific API Compatibility Layer\nCreate &#96;utils/compat.py&#96; to:\n- Provide version-specific API wrappers\n- Handle API differences between versions\n- Export compatibility functions that can be used throughout the codebase\n- Include placeholders for known API differences (to be filled in based on user's specific requirements)\n\n### 4. Update Manifest\nModify &#96;blender_manifest.toml&#96; to:\n- Set &#96;blender_version_min = \"4.2.0\"&#96; to support all three versions\n- Keep existing metadata structure\n\n### 5. Registration Logic\nUpdate registration in &#96;__init__.py&#96;:\n- Ensure registration works across all versions\n- Add version checks if needed for version-specific registration steps\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Files to Create\n- &#96;utils/__init__.py&#96; - Initialize utils package\n- &#96;utils/version.py&#96; - Version detection and comparison utilities\n- &#96;utils/compat.py&#96; - API compatibility layer for version-specific code\n\n### Files to Modify\n- &#96;__init__.py&#96; - Add bl_info and version-aware registration\n- &#96;blender_manifest.toml&#96; - Update minimum version requirement\n\n## Testing Strategy\n- Test registration in each Blender version\n- Verify metadata is correctly recognized\n- Test core functionality across versions\n- Add version-specific API tests as differences are identified",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Create utils package with __init__.py",
      "id": "create_utils_package",
      "status": "pending"
    },

        "create_utils_package"
      ],

      "status": "pending"
    },

        "create_version_module"
      ],
      "id": "create_compat_module",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Add bl_info dictionary to __init__.py for 4.2 LTS compatibility",
      "dependencies": [
        "create_version_module"
      ],
      "id": "add_bl_info",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Update blender_manifest.toml to set minimum version to 4.2.0",
      "id": "update_manifest",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {

      "dependencies": [
        "add_bl_info",
        "create_version_module"
      ],
      "id": "update_registration",

    }
  ]
}
```

---

_**User (2025-12-01 19:37:04Z)**_

The addon was last maintained in 2019. It was built for 2.83. Since then, blender added geometry nodes, which is the major feature that atomic does not support. We updated several operators for 4.5, but never for 5.0. The other API changes must be discovered. We can checkout 0eda0a88eb7261720cee3353a33b99725e99d81d if we need to see the original code.

---

```json
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef material_geometry_nodes(material_key):\n    # returns a list of object keys that use the material via Geometry Nodes\n\n    users = []\n    material = bpy.data.materials[material_key]\n\n    for obj in bpy.data.objects:\n        if hasattr(obj, 'modifiers'):\n            for modifier in obj.modifiers:\n                if modifier.type == 'NODES' and hasattr(modifier, 'node_group') and modifier.node_group:\n                    ng = modifier.node_group\n                    if node_group_has_material(ng.name, material.name):\n                        users.append(obj.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_objects(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list of object keys that use this node group via Geometry Nodes modifiers\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # node groups that use this node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    for obj in bpy.data.objects:\n        if hasattr(obj, 'modifiers'):\n            for modifier in obj.modifiers:\n                if modifier.type == 'NODES' and hasattr(modifier, 'node_group') and modifier.node_group:\n                    ng = modifier.node_group\n                    if ng.name == node_group.name or ng.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(obj.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_objects(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list of object keys that use this node group via Geometry Nodes modifiers\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # node groups that use this node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    for obj in bpy.data.objects:\n        if hasattr(obj, 'modifiers'):\n            for modifier in obj.modifiers:\n                if modifier.type == 'NODES' and hasattr(modifier, 'node_group') and modifier.node_group:\n                    ng = modifier.node_group\n                    if ng.name == node_group.name or ng.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(obj.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef image_geometry_nodes(image_key):\n    # returns a list of object keys that use the image through Geometry Nodes\n\n    users = []\n    image = bpy.data.images[image_key]\n\n    # list of node groups that use this image\n    node_group_users = image_node_groups(image_key)\n\n    for obj in bpy.data.objects:\n        # check Geometry Nodes modifiers\n        if hasattr(obj, 'modifiers'):\n            for modifier in obj.modifiers:\n                if modifier.type == 'NODES' and hasattr(modifier, 'node_group') and modifier.node_group:\n                    ng = modifier.node_group\n                    # direct usage in the modifier's tree\n                    if node_group_has_image(ng.name, image.name):\n                        users.append(obj.name)\n                    # usage via nested node groups\n                    elif ng.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(obj.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef image_geometry_nodes(image_key):\n    # returns a list of object keys that use the image through Geometry Nodes\n\n    users = []\n    image = bpy.data.images[image_key]\n\n    # list of node groups that use this image\n    node_group_users = image_node_groups(image_key)\n\n    for obj in bpy.data.objects:\n        # check Geometry Nodes modifiers\n        if hasattr(obj, 'modifiers'):\n            for modifier in obj.modifiers:\n                if modifier.type == 'NODES' and hasattr(modifier, 'node_group') and modifier.node_group:\n                    ng = modifier.node_group\n                    # direct usage in the modifier's tree\n                    if node_group_has_image(ng.name, image.name):\n                        users.append(obj.name)\n                    # usage via nested node groups\n                    elif ng.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(obj.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_has_node_group(search_group_key, node_group_key):\n    # returns true if a node group contains this node group\n\n    has_node_group = False\n    search_group = bpy.data.node_groups[search_group_key]\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # for each node in our search group\n    for node in search_group.nodes:\n\n        # if node is a node group and has a valid node tree\n        if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n            if node.node_tree.name == \"RG_MetallicMap\":\n                print(node.node_tree.name)\n                print(node_group.name)\n\n            # base case\n            # if node group is our node group\n            if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                has_node_group = True\n\n            # recurse case\n            # if node group is any other node group\n            else:\n                has_node_group = node_group_has_node_group(\n                    node.node_tree.name, node_group.name)\n\n        # break the loop if the node group is found\n        if has_node_group:\n            break\n\n    return has_node_group",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_has_node_group(search_group_key, node_group_key):\n    # returns true if a node group contains this node group\n\n    has_node_group = False\n    search_group = bpy.data.node_groups[search_group_key]\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # for each node in our search group\n    for node in search_group.nodes:\n\n        # if node is a node group and has a valid node tree\n        if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n            if node.node_tree.name == \"RG_MetallicMap\":\n                print(node.node_tree.name)\n                print(node_group.name)\n\n            # base case\n            # if node group is our node group\n            if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                has_node_group = True\n\n            # recurse case\n            # if node group is any other node group\n            else:\n                has_node_group = node_group_has_node_group(\n                    node.node_tree.name, node_group.name)\n\n        # break the loop if the node group is found\n        if has_node_group:\n            break\n\n    return has_node_group",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_has_material(node_group_key, material_key):\n    # returns true if a node group contains this material (directly or nested)\n\n    has_material = False\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n    material = bpy.data.materials[material_key]\n\n    for node in node_group.nodes:\n        # base case: nodes with a material property (e.g., Set Material)\n        if hasattr(node, 'material') and node.material:\n            if node.material.name == material.name:\n                has_material = True\n\n        # recurse case: nested node groups\n        elif hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n            has_material = node_group_has_material(\n                node.node_tree.name, material.name)\n\n        if has_material:\n            break\n\n    return has_material",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_has_material(node_group_key, material_key):\n    # returns true if a node group contains this material (directly or nested)\n\n    has_material = False\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n    material = bpy.data.materials[material_key]\n\n    for node in node_group.nodes:\n        # base case: nodes with a material property (e.g., Set Material)\n        if hasattr(node, 'material') and node.material:\n            if node.material.name == material.name:\n                has_material = True\n\n        # recurse case: nested node groups\n        elif hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n            has_material = node_group_has_material(\n                node.node_tree.name, material.name)\n\n        if has_material:\n            break\n\n    return has_material",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 4,
            "line": 716
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 655
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "stats/users.py"
      },
      "score": 0.3158990144729614
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_materials(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list of material keys that use the node group in their\n    # node trees\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # node groups that use this node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    for material in bpy.data.materials:\n\n        # if material uses nodes and has a valid node tree, check each node\n        if material.use_nodes and material.node_tree:\n            for node in material.node_tree.nodes:\n\n                # if node is a group node\n                if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n                    # if node is the node group\n                    if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                        users.append(material.name)\n\n                    # if node is using a node group contains our node group\n                    if node.node_tree.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(material.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_materials(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list of material keys that use the node group in their\n    # node trees\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # node groups that use this node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    for material in bpy.data.materials:\n\n        # if material uses nodes and has a valid node tree, check each node\n        if material.use_nodes and material.node_tree:\n            for node in material.node_tree.nodes:\n\n                # if node is a group node\n                if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n                    # if node is the node group\n                    if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                        users.append(material.name)\n\n                    # if node is using a node group contains our node group\n                    if node.node_tree.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(material.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_worlds(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list of world keys that use the node group in their node\n    # trees\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # node groups that use this node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    for world in bpy.data.worlds:\n\n        # if world uses nodes and has a valid node tree\n        if world.use_nodes and world.node_tree:\n            for node in world.node_tree.nodes:\n\n                # if node is a node group and has a valid node tree\n                if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n                    # if this node is our node group\n                    if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                        users.append(world.name)\n\n                    # if this node is one of the node groups that use\n                    # our node group\n                    elif node.node_tree.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(world.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_worlds(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list of world keys that use the node group in their node\n    # trees\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # node groups that use this node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    for world in bpy.data.worlds:\n\n        # if world uses nodes and has a valid node tree\n        if world.use_nodes and world.node_tree:\n            for node in world.node_tree.nodes:\n\n                # if node is a node group and has a valid node tree\n                if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n                    # if this node is our node group\n                    if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                        users.append(world.name)\n\n                    # if this node is one of the node groups that use\n                    # our node group\n                    elif node.node_tree.name in node_group_users:\n                        users.append(world.name)\n\n    return distinct(users)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\n# Atomic Data Manager Inspect Node Groups UI Operator\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_inspect_node_groups(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Inspect Node Groups\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.inspect_node_groups\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Inspect Node Groups\"\r\n\r\n    # user lists\r\n    users_compositors = []\r\n    users_materials = []\r\n    users_node_groups = []\r\n    users_textures = []\r\n    users_worlds = []\r\n\r\n    def draw(self, context):\r\n        global inspection_update_trigger\r\n        atom = bpy.context.scene.atomic\r\n\r\n        layout = self.layout\r\n\r\n        # inspect node groups header\r\n        ui_layouts.inspect_header(\r\n            layout=layout,\r\n            atom_prop=\"node_groups_field\",\r\n            data=\"node_groups\"\r\n        )\r\n\r\n        # inspection update code\r\n        if inspection_update_trigger:\r\n\r\n            # if key is valid, update the user lists\r\n            if atom.node_groups_field in bpy.data.node_groups.keys():\r\n\r\n                self.users_compositors = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_compositors(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_materials = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_materials(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_node_groups = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_node_groups(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_textures = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_textures(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_worlds = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_worlds(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n\r\n            # if key is invalid, empty the user lists\r\n            else:\r\n                self.users_compositors = []\r\n                self.users_materials = []\r\n                self.users_node_groups = []\r\n                self.users_textures = []\r\n                self.users_worlds = []\r\n\r\n            inspection_update_trigger = False",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\n# Atomic Data Manager Inspect Node Groups UI Operator\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_inspect_node_groups(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Inspect Node Groups\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.inspect_node_groups\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Inspect Node Groups\"\r\n\r\n    # user lists\r\n    users_compositors = []\r\n    users_materials = []\r\n    users_node_groups = []\r\n    users_textures = []\r\n    users_worlds = []\r\n\r\n    def draw(self, context):\r\n        global inspection_update_trigger\r\n        atom = bpy.context.scene.atomic\r\n\r\n        layout = self.layout\r\n\r\n        # inspect node groups header\r\n        ui_layouts.inspect_header(\r\n            layout=layout,\r\n            atom_prop=\"node_groups_field\",\r\n            data=\"node_groups\"\r\n        )\r\n\r\n        # inspection update code\r\n        if inspection_update_trigger:\r\n\r\n            # if key is valid, update the user lists\r\n            if atom.node_groups_field in bpy.data.node_groups.keys():\r\n\r\n                self.users_compositors = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_compositors(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_materials = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_materials(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_node_groups = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_node_groups(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_textures = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_textures(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n                self.users_worlds = \\\r\n                    users.node_group_worlds(atom.node_groups_field)\r\n\r\n            # if key is invalid, empty the user lists\r\n            else:\r\n                self.users_compositors = []\r\n                self.users_materials = []\r\n                self.users_node_groups = []\r\n                self.users_textures = []\r\n                self.users_worlds = []\r\n\r\n            inspection_update_trigger = False",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_has_image(node_group_key, image_key):\n    # recursively returns true if the node group contains this image\n    # directly or if it contains a node group a node group that contains\n    # the image indirectly\n\n    has_image = False\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n    image = bpy.data.images[image_key]\n\n    # for each node in our search group\n    for node in node_group.nodes:\n\n        # base case\n        # if node has a not none image attribute\n        if hasattr(node, 'image') and node.image:\n\n            # if the node group is our node group\n            if node.image.name == image.name:\n                has_image = True\n\n        # recurse case\n        # if node is a node group and has a valid node tree\n        elif hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n            has_image = node_group_has_image(\n                node.node_tree.name, image.name)\n\n        # break the loop if the image is found\n        if has_image:\n            break\n\n    return has_image",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_has_image(node_group_key, image_key):\n    # recursively returns true if the node group contains this image\n    # directly or if it contains a node group a node group that contains\n    # the image indirectly\n\n    has_image = False\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n    image = bpy.data.images[image_key]\n\n    # for each node in our search group\n    for node in node_group.nodes:\n\n        # base case\n        # if node has a not none image attribute\n        if hasattr(node, 'image') and node.image:\n\n            # if the node group is our node group\n            if node.image.name == image.name:\n                has_image = True\n\n        # recurse case\n        # if node is a node group and has a valid node tree\n        elif hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n            has_image = node_group_has_image(\n                node.node_tree.name, image.name)\n\n        # break the loop if the image is found\n        if has_image:\n            break\n\n    return has_image",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_compositors(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list containing \"Compositor\" if the node group is used in\n    # the scene's compositor\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # a list of node groups that use our node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    # if our compositor uses nodes and has a valid node tree\n    if bpy.context.scene.use_nodes and bpy.context.scene.node_tree:\n\n        # check each node in the compositor\n        for node in bpy.context.scene.node_tree.nodes:\n\n            # if the node is a group and has a valid node tree\n            if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n                # if the node group is our node group\n                if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                    users.append(\"Compositor\")\n\n                # if the node group is in our list of node group users\n                if node.node_tree.name in node_group_users:\n                    users.append(\"Compositor\")\n\n    return distinct(users)",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 460,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef node_group_compositors(node_group_key):\n    # returns a list containing \"Compositor\" if the node group is used in\n    # the scene's compositor\n\n    users = []\n    node_group = bpy.data.node_groups[node_group_key]\n\n    # a list of node groups that use our node group\n    node_group_users = node_group_node_groups(node_group_key)\n\n    # if our compositor uses nodes and has a valid node tree\n    if bpy.context.scene.use_nodes and bpy.context.scene.node_tree:\n\n        # check each node in the compositor\n        for node in bpy.context.scene.node_tree.nodes:\n\n            # if the node is a group and has a valid node tree\n            if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:\n\n                # if the node group is our node group\n                if node.node_tree.name == node_group.name:\n                    users.append(\"Compositor\")\n\n                # if the node group is in our list of node group users\n                if node.node_tree.name in node_group_users:\n                    users.append(\"Compositor\")\n\n    return distinct(users)",
        "range": {

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\n# Atomic Data Manager Nuke Node Groups Operator\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_nuke_node_groups(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Remove all node groups from this project\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.nuke_node_groups\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Nuke Node Groups\"\r\n\r\n    def draw(self, context):\r\n        layout = self.layout\r\n\r\n        row = layout.row()\r\n        row.label(text=\"Remove the following data-blocks?\")\r\n\r\n        node_groups = bpy.data.node_groups.keys()\r\n        ui_layouts.box_list(\r\n            layout=layout,\r\n            items=node_groups,\r\n            icon=\"NODETREE\"\r\n        )\r\n\r\n        row = layout.row()  # extra space\r\n\r\n    def execute(self, context):\r\n        nuke.node_groups()\r\n        return {'FINISHED'}\r\n\r\n    def invoke(self, context, event):\r\n        wm = context.window_manager\r\n        return wm.invoke_props_dialog(self)",
```

```json
{
  "name": "Multi-Blender Version Support",
  "overview": "Add support for Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0 in the same codebase by implementing version detection, dual metadata format support (bl_info and blender_manifest.toml), and a framework for version-specific API handling.",
  "plan": "# Multi-Blender Version Support Implementation\n\n## Overview\nImplement multi-version support for Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0. The addon was originally built for 2.83 (2019), updated for 4.5, but not yet for 5.0. Geometry nodes support exists but may need enhancement. API differences between versions need to be discovered and handled.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n### 1. Version Detection Module\nCreate &#96;utils/version.py&#96; to:\n- Detect Blender version using &#96;bpy.app.version&#96; (tuple: major, minor, patch)\n- Provide version comparison utilities (&#96;is_version_at_least()&#96;, &#96;get_version_string()&#96;)\n- Define version constants: VERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0), VERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0), VERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)\n- Export &#96;get_blender_version()&#96; that returns normalized version tuple\n- Export &#96;get_version_category()&#96; that returns '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0'\n\n### 2. Dual Metadata Format Support\nUpdate &#96;__init__.py&#96; to:\n- Add &#96;bl_info&#96; dictionary for Blender 4.2 LTS compatibility (required for 4.2)\n- Keep existing &#96;blender_manifest.toml&#96; for 4.5 LTS and 5.0 (required for 4.5+)\n- Use version detection to conditionally define bl_info only for 4.2\n- Ensure both formats contain consistent metadata (name, version, author, etc.)\n\n### 3. Version-Specific API Compatibility Layer\nCreate &#96;utils/compat.py&#96; to:\n- Provide version-specific API wrappers for discovered differences\n- Handle API differences between 4.2, 4.5, and 5.0\n- Export compatibility functions (e.g., &#96;safe_register_class()&#96;, &#96;get_addon_prefs()&#96;)\n- Include framework for geometry nodes API differences if any\n- Add placeholders for operator registration differences\n\n### 4. Update Manifest\nModify &#96;blender_manifest.toml&#96; to:\n- Set &#96;blender_version_min = \"4.2.0\"&#96; to support all three versions\n- Keep existing metadata structure for 4.5/5.0\n\n### 5. Operator Compatibility\nReview and update operators in &#96;ops/&#96;:\n- Check for version-specific operator registration issues\n- Ensure operators work across 4.2, 4.5, and 5.0\n- Add version checks where API differences are discovered\n\n### 6. Geometry Nodes Support\nReview existing geometry nodes code in &#96;stats/users.py&#96;:\n- Verify geometry nodes detection works across all versions\n- Check for API changes in modifier.node_group access\n- Ensure compatibility with 4.2, 4.5, and 5.0 geometry nodes API\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Files to Create\n- &#96;utils/__init__.py&#96; - Initialize utils package\n- &#96;utils/version.py&#96; - Version detection and comparison utilities\n- &#96;utils/compat.py&#96; - API compatibility layer for version-specific code\n\n### Files to Modify\n- &#96;__init__.py&#96; - Add conditional bl_info and version-aware registration\n- &#96;blender_manifest.toml&#96; - Update minimum version to 4.2.0\n- &#96;ops/__init__.py&#96; - Review operator registration for version compatibility\n- &#96;stats/users.py&#96; - Verify geometry nodes code works across versions\n\n## Discovery and Testing Strategy\n- Test registration in each Blender version (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, 5.0)\n- Verify metadata is correctly recognized in each version\n- Test core functionality (nuke, clean, inspect) across versions\n- Discover API differences through testing and add to compat.py\n- Verify geometry nodes detection works in all versions\n- Document discovered API differences for future reference",
```

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 127, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,127 @@
- 
+ """
+ Copyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative
+ 
+ This file is part of Atomic Data Manager.
+ 
+ Atomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute
+ it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
+ as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the
+ License, or (at your option) any later version.
+ 
+ Atomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will
+ be useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty
+ of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
+ General Public License for more details.
+ 
+ You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along
+ with Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see &lt;https://www.gnu.org/licenses/&gt;.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ This module provides version detection and comparison utilities for
+ multi-version Blender support (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0).
+ 
+ """
+ 
+ import bpy
+ 
+ # Version constants
+ VERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0)
+ VERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0)
+ VERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)
+ 
+ 
+ def get_blender_version():
+     """
+     Returns the current Blender version as a tuple (major, minor, patch).
+     
+     Returns:
+         tuple: (major, minor, patch) version numbers
+     """
+     return bpy.app.version
+ 
+ 
+ def get_version_string():
+     """
+     Returns the current Blender version as a string (e.g., "4.2.0").
+     
+     Returns:
+         str: Version string in format "major.minor.patch"
+     """
+     version = get_blender_version()
+     return f"{version[0]}.{version[1]}.{version[2]}"
+ 
+ 
+ def is_version_at_least(major, minor=0, patch=0):
+     """
+     Check if the current Blender version is at least the specified version.
+     
+     Args:
+         major (int): Major version number
+         minor (int): Minor version number (default: 0)
+         patch (int): Patch version number (default: 0)
+     
+     Returns:
+         bool: True if current version &gt;= specified version
+     """
+     current = get_blender_version()
+     target = (major, minor, patch)
+     
+     if current[0] != target[0]:
+         return current[0] &gt; target[0]
+     if current[1] != target[1]:
+         return current[1] &gt; target[1]
+     return current[2] &gt;= target[2]
+ 
+ 
+ def is_version_less_than(major, minor=0, patch=0):
+     """
+     Check if the current Blender version is less than the specified version.
+     
+     Args:
+         major (int): Major version number
+         minor (int): Minor version number (default: 0)
+         patch (int): Patch version number (default: 0)
+     
+     Returns:
+         bool: True if current version &lt; specified version
+     """
+     return not is_version_at_least(major, minor, patch)
+ 
+ 
+ def get_version_category():
+     """
+     Returns the version category string for the current Blender version.
+     
+     Returns:
+         str: '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0' based on the current version
+     """
+     version = get_blender_version()
+     major, minor = version[0], version[1]
+     
+     if major == 4:
+         if minor &lt; 5:
+             return '4.2'
+         else:
+             return '4.5'
+     elif major &gt;= 5:
+         return '5.0'
+     else:
+         # Fallback for older versions
+         return f"{major}.{minor}"
+ 
+ 
+ def is_version_4_2():
+     """Check if running Blender 4.2 LTS."""
+     return is_version_at_least(4, 2, 0) and is_version_less_than(4, 5, 0)
+ 
+ 
+ def is_version_4_5():
+     """Check if running Blender 4.5 LTS."""
+     return is_version_at_least(4, 5, 0) and is_version_less_than(5, 0, 0)
+ 
+ 
+ def is_version_5_0():
+     """Check if running Blender 5.0 or later."""
+     return is_version_at_least(5, 0, 0)
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 131, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,131 @@
- 
+ """
+ Copyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative
+ 
+ This file is part of Atomic Data Manager.
+ 
+ Atomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute
+ it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
+ as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the
+ License, or (at your option) any later version.
+ 
+ Atomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will
+ be useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty
+ of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU
+ General Public License for more details.
+ 
+ You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along
+ with Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see &lt;https://www.gnu.org/licenses/&gt;.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ This module provides API compatibility functions for handling differences
+ between Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0.
+ 
+ """
+ 
+ import bpy
+ from bpy.utils import register_class, unregister_class
+ from . import version
+ 
+ 
+ def safe_register_class(cls):
+     """
+     Safely register a class, handling any version-specific registration issues.
+     
+     Args:
+         cls: The class to register
+     
+     Returns:
+         bool: True if registration succeeded, False otherwise
+     """
+     try:
+         register_class(cls)
+         return True
+     except Exception as e:
+         print(f"Warning: Failed to register {cls.__name__}: {e}")
+         return False
+ 
+ 
+ def safe_unregister_class(cls):
+     """
+     Safely unregister a class, handling any version-specific unregistration issues.
+     
+     Args:
+         cls: The class to unregister
+     
+     Returns:
+         bool: True if unregistration succeeded, False otherwise
+     """
+     try:
+         unregister_class(cls)
+         return True
+     except Exception as e:
+         print(f"Warning: Failed to unregister {cls.__name__}: {e}")
+         return False
+ 
+ 
+ def get_addon_prefs():
+     """
+     Get the addon preferences instance, compatible across versions.
+     
+     Returns:
+         AddonPreferences or None: The addon preferences instance if found
+     """
+     prefs = bpy.context.preferences
+     for addon in prefs.addons.values():
+         ap = getattr(addon, "preferences", None)
+         if ap and hasattr(ap, "enable_missing_file_warning"):
+             return ap
+     return None
+ 
+ 
+ def get_geometry_nodes_modifier_node_group(modifier):
+     """
+     Get the node group from a geometry nodes modifier, handling version differences.
+     
+     Args:
+         modifier: The modifier object
+     
+     Returns:
+         NodeGroup or None: The node group if available
+     """
+     if not hasattr(modifier, 'type') or modifier.type != 'NODES':
+         return None
+     
+     # Check for node_group attribute (available in all supported versions)
+     if hasattr(modifier, 'node_group') and modifier.node_group:
+         return modifier.node_group
+     
+     return None
+ 
+ 
+ def is_geometry_nodes_modifier(modifier):
+     """
+     Check if a modifier is a geometry nodes modifier, compatible across versions.
+     
+     Args:
+         modifier: The modifier object
+     
+     Returns:
+         bool: True if the modifier is a geometry nodes modifier
+     """
+     if not hasattr(modifier, 'type'):
+         return False
+     
+     return modifier.type == 'NODES'
+ 
+ 
+ def get_node_tree_from_node(node):
+     """
+     Get the node tree from a node, handling version differences.
+     
+     Args:
+         node: The node object
+     
+     Returns:
+         NodeTree or None: The node tree if available
+     """
+     if hasattr(node, 'node_tree') and node.node_tree:
+         return node.node_tree
+     return None
+ 
```

```json
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the direct use operators, intended to be used with\r\nAtomic's pie menu interface. However, they can be implemented anywhere\r\nif they need to be.\r\n\r\nThese operators basically wrap the functions from ops.utils.nuke.py and\r\nops.utils.clean.py into operators so they can be easily called by other\r\nintefaces in Blender.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\nfrom ..stats import unused\r\nfrom .utils import nuke\r\nfrom .utils import clean\r\nfrom ..ui.utils import ui_layouts\r\n\r\n\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_invoke_pie_menu_ui(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Invokes Atomic's pie menu UI if the \\\"Enable Pie Menu UI\\\"\r\n    preference is enabled in Atomic's preferences panel\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.invoke_pie_menu_ui\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Invoke Pie Menu UI\"\r\n\r\n    def execute(self, context):\r\n        if config.enable_pie_menu_ui:\r\n            bpy.ops.wm.call_menu_pie(name=\"ATOMIC_MT_main_pie\")\r\n        return {'FINISHED'}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"\"\"\r"

            "lineNumber": 2,

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the direct use operators, intended to be used with\r\nAtomic's pie menu interface. However, they can be implemented anywhere\r\nif they need to be.\r\n\r\nThese operators basically wrap the functions from ops.utils.nuke.py and\r\nops.utils.clean.py into operators so they can be easily called by other\r\nintefaces in Blender.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\nfrom ..stats import unused\r\nfrom .utils import nuke\r\nfrom .utils import clean\r\nfrom ..ui.utils import ui_layouts\r\n\r\n\r\nclass ATOMIC_OT_invoke_pie_menu_ui(bpy.types.Operator):\r\n    \"\"\"Invokes Atomic's pie menu UI if the \\\"Enable Pie Menu UI\\\"\r\n    preference is enabled in Atomic's preferences panel\"\"\"\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic.invoke_pie_menu_ui\"\r\n    bl_label = \"Invoke Pie Menu UI\"\r\n\r\n    def execute(self, context):\r\n        if config.enable_pie_menu_ui:\r\n            bpy.ops.wm.call_menu_pie(name=\"ATOMIC_MT_main_pie\")\r\n        return {'FINISHED'}",
```

_**Agent (default)**_


## Summary of Changes

1. **Created utils package** (`utils/__init__.py`) — Initialized the package with version and compat modules.

2. **Version detection module** (`utils/version.py`) — Added:
   - Version constants for 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0
   - `get_blender_version()` — Returns version tuple
   - `get_version_string()` — Returns version string
   - `is_version_at_least()` / `is_version_less_than()` — Comparison utilities
   - `get_version_category()` — Returns '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0'
   - Helper functions: `is_version_4_2()`, `is_version_4_5()`, `is_version_5_0()`

3. **Compatibility layer** (`utils/compat.py`) — Added:
   - `safe_register_class()` / `safe_unregister_class()` — Safe registration wrappers
   - `get_addon_prefs()` — Version-safe preferences access
   - `get_geometry_nodes_modifier_node_group()` — Safe geometry nodes access
   - `is_geometry_nodes_modifier()` — Version-safe modifier checking
   - `get_node_tree_from_node()` — Safe node tree access

4. **Dual metadata format support** — Updated `__init__.py`:
   - Added conditional `bl_info` dictionary (only for Blender < 4.5)
   - `bl_info` is required for 4.2 LTS, while `blender_manifest.toml` is used for 4.5+

5. **Updated manifest** — Changed `blender_version_min` from "4.5.0" to "4.2.0" in `blender_manifest.toml`

6. **Geometry nodes compatibility** — Updated `stats/users.py`:
   - Updated `image_geometry_nodes()`, `material_geometry_nodes()`, and `node_group_objects()` to use compatibility functions
   - Ensures geometry nodes detection works across 4.2, 4.5, and 5.0

7. **Operator review** — Reviewed all operators; they use standard Blender API patterns compatible across versions

All todos are complete. The addon supports Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0 with version detection and compatibility handling. The codebase is ready for testing across these versions.

_**Agent (default)**_

**Yes, it should run on Blender 4.3.**

Here's what happens:

1. **No `bl_info` conflict**: `bl_info` is only defined for 4.2.x (not 4.3 or 4.4), so there's no conflict.

2. **`blender_manifest.toml` will be used**: The manifest has `blender_version_min = "4.2.0"`, so Blender 4.3 qualifies and will use the manifest format.

3. **API compatibility**: 4.3 is between 4.2 and 4.5, so the API should be compatible. The compatibility functions handle version differences.

The fix ensures:
- **4.2 LTS**: Uses `bl_info` (required for 4.2)
- **4.3**: Uses `blender_manifest.toml` only (no `bl_info` conflict)
- **4.5+**: Uses `blender_manifest.toml` only

It should work on 4.3. If you encounter issues, we can add version-specific handling.

If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the Atomic preferences UI, preferences properties, and\r\nsome functions for syncing the preference properties with external factors.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\n# updater removed in Blender 4.5 extension format\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _get_addon_prefs():\r\n    # robustly find our AddonPreferences instance regardless of module name\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"bl_idname\") and ap.bl_idname == ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel.bl_idname:\r\n            return ap\r\n        # fallback: match by known property\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

```json
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_addon_prefs():\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Get the addon preferences instance, compatible across versions.\r\n    \r\n    Returns:\r\n        AddonPreferences or None: The addon preferences instance if found\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\n\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.         "originalContents": "\"\"\"\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\n\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\nGeneral Public License for more details.\n\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\n\n---\n\nThis module provides version detection and comparison utilities for\nmulti-version Blender support (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0).\n\n\"\"\"\n\nimport bpy\n\n# Version constants\nVERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0)\nVERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0)\nVERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)\n\n\ndef get_blender_version():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the current Blender version as a tuple (major, minor, patch).\n    \n    Returns:\n        tuple: (major, minor, patch) version numbers\n    \"\"\"\n    return bpy.app.version",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis module provides API compatibility functions for handling differences\r\nbetween Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class, unregister_class\r\nfrom . import version\r\n\r\n\r\ndef safe_register_class(cls):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Safely register a class, handling any version-specific registration issues.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        cls: The class to register\r\n    \r\n    Returns:\r\n        bool: True if registration succeeded, False otherwise\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        register_class(cls)\r\n        return True\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to register {cls.__name__}: {e}\")\r\n        return False",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis module provides API compatibility functions for handling differences\r\nbetween Blender 4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class, unregister_class\r\nfrom . import version\r\n\r\n\r\ndef safe_register_class(cls):\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Safely register a class, handling any version-specific registration issues.\r\n    \r\n    Args:\r\n        cls: The class to register\r\n    \r\n    Returns:\r\n        bool: True if registration succeeded, False otherwise\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        register_class(cls)\r\n        return True\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Warning: Failed to register {cls.__name__}: {e}\")\r\n        return False",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef get_version_string():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the current Blender version as a string (e.g., \"4.2.0\").\n    \n    Returns:\n        str: Version string in format \"major.minor.patch\"\n    \"\"\"\n    version = get_blender_version()\n    return f\"{version[0]}.{version[1]}.{version[2]}\"",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef get_version_category():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the version category string for the current Blender version.\n    \n    Returns:\n        str: '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0' based on the current version\n    \"\"\"\n    version = get_blender_version()\n    major, minor = version[0], version[1]\n    \n    if major == 4:\n        if minor \u003c 5:\n            return '4.2'\n        else:\n            return '4.5'\n    elif major \u003e= 5:\n        return '5.0'\n    else:\n        # Fallback for older versions\n        return f\"{major}.{minor}\"",

            "lineNumber": 88,

            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 89,

            "lineNumber": 95,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "    Returns:"
          },

            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "        str: '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0' based on the current version"

            "lineNumber": 98,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "    version = get_blender_version()"

            "lineNumber": 100,

            "lineNumber": 101,
```
