          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef get_version_category():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the version category string for the current Blender version.\n    \n    Returns:\n        str: '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0' based on the current version\n    \"\"\"\n    version = get_blender_version()\n    major, minor = version[0], version[1]\n    \n    if major == 4:\n        if minor \u003c 5:\n            return '4.2'\n        else:\n            return '4.5'\n    elif major \u003e= 5:\n        return '5.0'\n    else:\n        # Fallback for older versions\n        return f\"{major}.{minor}\"",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 116
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 76
          }

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file handles the registration of the atomic_data_manager.ui package\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nfrom . import main_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import stats_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import inspect_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import pie_menu_ui\r\nfrom . import preferences_ui\r\nfrom . import support_me_ui\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    # register preferences first so we can access variables in config.py\r\n    preferences_ui.register()\r\n\r\n    # register everything else\r\n    main_panel_ui.register()\r\n    stats_panel_ui.register()\r\n    inspect_ui.register()\r\n    missing_file_ui.register()\r\n    pie_menu_ui.register()\r\n    support_me_ui.register()",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions that detect missing files in the Blender\r\nproject.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nimport os\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_missing(data):\r\n    # returns a list of keys of unpacked data-blocks with non-existent\r\n    # filepaths\r\n\r\n    missing = []\r\n\r\n    # list of keys that should not be flagged\r\n    do_not_flag = [\"Render Result\", \"Viewer Node\", \"D-NOISE Export\"]\r\n\r\n    for datablock in data:\r\n\r\n        # the absolute path to our data-block\r\n        abspath = bpy.path.abspath(datablock.filepath)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is not packed and has an invalid filepath\r\n        if not datablock.packed_files and not os.path.isfile(abspath):\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is packed but it does not have a filepath\r\n        elif datablock.packed_files and not abspath:\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n    return missing",
        "detailedLines": [

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\nreg_list = [ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel]\r\nkeymaps = []\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    for cls in reg_list:\r\n        register_class(cls)\r\n\r\n    # make sure global preferences are updated on registration\r\n    copy_prefs_to_config(None, None)\r\n\r\n    # update keymaps\r\n    add_pie_menu_hotkeys()",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "class ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel(bpy.types.AddonPreferences):\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 236,
            "text": "def draw(self, context):\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 305,
            "text": "  # extra space\r"
          },
          {

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\nreg_list = [ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel]\r\nkeymaps = []\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    for cls in reg_list:\r\n        register_class(cls)\r\n\r\n    # make sure global preferences are updated on registration\r\n    copy_prefs_to_config(None, None)\r\n\r\n    # update keymaps\r\n    add_pie_menu_hotkeys()",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains global copies of Atomic's preferences so that they\r\ncan be easily access throughout the add-on.\r\n\r\nNOTE:\r\nChanging the values of these variables will NOT change the values in the\r\nAtomic's preferences. If you want to change a setting, change it in\r\nBlender, not in here.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\n# visible atomic preferences\r\nenable_missing_file_warning = True\r\nenable_support_me_popup = True\r\ninclude_fake_users = False\r\nenable_pie_menu_ui = True\r\n\r\n# hidden atomic preferences\r\npie_menu_type = \"D\"\r\npie_menu_alt = False\r\npie_menu_any = False\r\npie_menu_ctrl = False\r\npie_menu_oskey = False\r\npie_menu_shift = False\r\nlast_popup_day = 0",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions that detect unnamed data-blocks in the\r\nBlender project.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nimport re\r\n\r\n\r\ndef collections():\r\n    # returns the keys of all unnamed collections in the project\r\n    unnamed = []\r\n\r\n    for collection in bpy.data.collections:\r\n        if re.match(r'.*\\.\\d\\d\\d$', collection.name) or \\\r\n                collection.name.startswith(\"Collection\"):\r\n            unnamed.append(collection.name)\r\n\r\n    return unnamed",

_**Agent (default)**_


The issue is that the `vscode_development` extension is using the folder name `atomic-data-manager` (hyphens) as the module name, but Python can't import modules with hyphens. The manifest correctly specifies `atomic_data_manager` (underscores).

This is a `vscode_development` configuration issue. The extension should use the module name from the manifest, not the folder name.

**The problem:**
- Your folder: `atomic-data-manager` (hyphens - fine for folder names)
- Python module needed: `atomic_data_manager` (underscores - required for Python)
- vscode_development is using: `atomic-data-manager` (wrong - has hyphens)

**Solutions:**

1. **Configure vscode_development** to use the correct module name from the manifest (`atomic_data_manager`) instead of the folder name.

2. **Rename your development folder** to `atomic_data_manager` (with underscores) to match the module name. This is the simplest fix.

3. **For production installs**, when users install the addon, they should install it as a folder named `atomic_data_manager` (with underscores), not `atomic-data-manager`.

The code is correct; this is a development environment configuration issue. The manifest correctly specifies `modules = ["atomic_data_manager"]`, which is what Blender will use when the addon is properly installed.

Should I help configure the vscode_development extension, or would you prefer to rename the folder?

---

_**User (2025-12-01 20:02:41Z)**_

            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "# Atomic Data Manager Properties"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "class ATOMIC_PG_main(bpy.types.PropertyGroup):"

          },

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\n\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\nGeneral Public License for more details.\n\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\n\n---\n\nThis file contains Atomic's global properties and handles the\nregistration for all packages within the add-on.\n\n\"\"\"\n\n\n__author__ = \"RaincloudTheDragon\"\n__maintainer__ = \"RaincloudTheDragon\"\n__version__ = \"1.1.0\"\n\n\nimport bpy\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\nfrom . import ops\nfrom . import ui\nfrom .ui import inspect_ui\nfrom .utils import version\n# updater removed for Blender 4.5 extension format\n\n# bl_info is required for Blender 4.2 LTS, replaced by blender_manifest.toml in 4.3+\n# Conditionally define bl_info only for 4.2 LTS (4.2.0 to 4.2.x)\nif version.is_version_4_2():\n    bl_info = {\n        \"name\": \"Atomic Data Manager\",\n        \"author\": \"RaincloudTheDragon\",\n        \"version\": (1, 1, 0),\n        \"blender\": (4, 2, 0),\n        \"location\": \"Properties \u003e Scene\",\n        \"description\": \"An Intelligent Data Manager for Blender.\",\n        \"warning\": \"\",\n        \"doc_url\": \"https://github.com/grantwilk/atomic-data-manager\",\n        \"category\": \"Utility\",\n    }",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 221
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "__init__.py"
      },
      "score": 0.26013505458831787

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains global copies of Atomic's preferences so that they\r\ncan be easily access throughout the add-on.\r\n\r\nNOTE:\r\nChanging the values of these variables will NOT change the values in the\r\nAtomic's preferences. If you want to change a setting, change it in\r\nBlender, not in here.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\n# visible atomic preferences\r\nenable_missing_file_warning = True\r\nenable_support_me_popup = True\r\ninclude_fake_users = False\r\nenable_pie_menu_ui = True\r\n\r\n# hidden atomic preferences\r\npie_menu_type = \"D\"\r\npie_menu_alt = False\r\npie_menu_any = False\r\npie_menu_ctrl = False\r\npie_menu_oskey = False\r\npie_menu_shift = False\r\nlast_popup_day = 0",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains global copies of Atomic's preferences so that they\r\ncan be easily access throughout the add-on.\r\n\r\nNOTE:\r\nChanging the values of these variables will NOT change the values in the\r\nAtomic's preferences. If you want to change a setting, change it in\r\nBlender, not in here.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\n# visible atomic preferences\r\nenable_missing_file_warning = True\r\nenable_support_me_popup = True\r\ninclude_fake_users = False\r\nenable_pie_menu_ui = True\r\n\r\n# hidden atomic preferences\r\npie_menu_type = \"D\"\r\npie_menu_alt = False\r\npie_menu_any = False\r\npie_menu_ctrl = False\r\npie_menu_oskey = False\r\npie_menu_shift = False\r\nlast_popup_day = 0",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the Atomic preferences UI, preferences properties, and\r\nsome functions for syncing the preference properties with external factors.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\n# updater removed in Blender 4.5 extension format\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _get_addon_prefs():\r\n    # robustly find our AddonPreferences instance regardless of module name\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"bl_idname\") and ap.bl_idname == ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel.bl_idname:\r\n            return ap\r\n        # fallback: match by known property\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the Atomic preferences UI, preferences properties, and\r\nsome functions for syncing the preference properties with external factors.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\n# updater removed in Blender 4.5 extension format\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _get_addon_prefs():\r\n    # robustly find our AddonPreferences instance regardless of module name\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"bl_idname\") and ap.bl_idname == ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel.bl_idname:\r\n            return ap\r\n        # fallback: match by known property\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file handles the registration of the atomic_data_manager.ui package\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nfrom . import main_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import stats_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import inspect_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import pie_menu_ui\r\nfrom . import preferences_ui\r\nfrom . import support_me_ui\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    # register preferences first so we can access variables in config.py\r\n    preferences_ui.register()\r\n\r\n    # register everything else\r\n    main_panel_ui.register()\r\n    stats_panel_ui.register()\r\n    inspect_ui.register()\r\n    missing_file_ui.register()\r\n    pie_menu_ui.register()\r\n    support_me_ui.register()",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.         "contents": "\"\"\"\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\n\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\nGeneral Public License for more details.\n\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\n\n---\n\nThis module provides version detection and comparison utilities for\nmulti-version Blender support (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0).\n\n\"\"\"\n\nimport bpy\n\n# Version constants\nVERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0)\nVERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0)\nVERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)\n\n\ndef get_blender_version():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the current Blender version as a tuple (major, minor, patch).\n    \n    Returns:\n        tuple: (major, minor, patch) version numbers\n    \"\"\"\n    return bpy.app.version",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\n\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\nGeneral Public License for more details.\n\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\n\n---\n\nThis module provides version detection and comparison utilities for\nmulti-version Blender support (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0).\n\n\"\"\"\n\nimport bpy\n\n# Version constants\nVERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0)\nVERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0)\nVERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)\n\n\ndef get_blender_version():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the current Blender version as a tuple (major, minor, patch).\n    \n    Returns:\n        tuple: (major, minor, patch) version numbers\n    \"\"\"\n    return bpy.app.version",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\nreg_list = [ATOMIC_OT_detect_missing]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    for item in reg_list:\r\n        register_class(item)\r\n\r\n    # run missing file auto-detection after loading a Blender file\r\n    bpy.app.handlers.load_post.append(autodetect_missing_files)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\n# Atomic Data Manager Preference Panel UI\r\nclass ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel(bpy.types.AddonPreferences):\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic_data_manager\"\r\n\r\n    # visible atomic preferences\r\n    enable_missing_file_warning: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Display a warning on startup if Atomic detects \"\r\n                    \"missing files in your project\",\r\n        default=True\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    enable_support_me_popup: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Occasionally display a popup asking if you would \"\r\n                    \"like to support Remington Creative\",\r\n        default=True\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    include_fake_users: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Include data-blocks with only fake users in unused \"\r\n                    \"data detection\",\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    enable_pie_menu_ui: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Enable the Atomic pie menu UI, so you can clean \"\r\n                    \"your project from anywhere.\",\r\n        default=True,\r\n        update=update_pie_menu_hotkeys\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # hidden atomic preferences\r\n    pie_menu_type: bpy.props.StringProperty(\r\n        default=\"D\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_alt: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_any: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_ctrl: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_oskey: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_shift: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    last_popup_day: bpy.props.FloatProperty(\r\n        default=0\r\n    )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\n# Atomic Data Manager Preference Panel UI\r\nclass ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel(bpy.types.AddonPreferences):\r\n    bl_idname = \"atomic_data_manager\"\r\n\r\n    # visible atomic preferences\r\n    enable_missing_file_warning: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Display a warning on startup if Atomic detects \"\r\n                    \"missing files in your project\",\r\n        default=True\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    enable_support_me_popup: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Occasionally display a popup asking if you would \"\r\n                    \"like to support Remington Creative\",\r\n        default=True\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    include_fake_users: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Include data-blocks with only fake users in unused \"\r\n                    \"data detection\",\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    enable_pie_menu_ui: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        description=\"Enable the Atomic pie menu UI, so you can clean \"\r\n                    \"your project from anywhere.\",\r\n        default=True,\r\n        update=update_pie_menu_hotkeys\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # hidden atomic preferences\r\n    pie_menu_type: bpy.props.StringProperty(\r\n        default=\"D\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_alt: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_any: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_ctrl: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_oskey: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    pie_menu_shift: bpy.props.BoolProperty(\r\n        default=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    last_popup_day: bpy.props.FloatProperty(\r\n        default=0\r\n    )",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef get_version_category():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the version category string for the current Blender version.\n    \n    Returns:\n        str: '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0' based on the current version\n    \"\"\"\n    version = get_blender_version()\n    major, minor = version[0], version[1]\n    \n    if major == 4:\n        if minor \u003c 5:\n            return '4.2'\n        else:\n            return '4.5'\n    elif major \u003e= 5:\n        return '5.0'\n    else:\n        # Fallback for older versions\n        return f\"{major}.{minor}\"",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "def is_version_less_than(major, minor=0, patch=0):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "    return not is_version_at_least(major, minor, patch)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90
          },

            "lineNumber": 91
          },

            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "    Returns:"
          },

            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "        str: '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0' based on the current version"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "    version = get_blender_version()"

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef get_version_category():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the version category string for the current Blender version.\n    \n    Returns:\n        str: '4.2', '4.5', or '5.0' based on the current version\n    \"\"\"\n    version = get_blender_version()\n    major, minor = version[0], version[1]\n    \n    if major == 4:\n        if minor \u003c 5:\n            return '4.2'\n        else:\n            return '4.5'\n    elif major \u003e= 5:\n        return '5.0'\n    else:\n        # Fallback for older versions\n        return f\"{major}.{minor}\"",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef add_pie_menu_hotkeys():\r\n    # adds the pie menu hotkeys to blender's addon keymaps\r\n\r\n    global keymaps\r\n    keyconfigs = bpy.context.window_manager.keyconfigs.addon\r\n\r\n    # check to see if a window keymap already exists\r\n    if \"Window\" in keyconfigs.keymaps.keys():\r\n        km = keyconfigs.keymaps['Window']\r\n\r\n    # if not, crate a new one\r\n    else:\r\n        km = keyconfigs.keymaps.new(\r\n            name=\"Window\",\r\n            space_type='EMPTY',\r\n            region_type='WINDOW'\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    # add a new keymap item to that keymap\r\n    kmi = km.keymap_items.new(\r\n        idname=\"atomic.invoke_pie_menu_ui\",\r\n        type=config.pie_menu_type,\r\n        value=\"PRESS\",\r\n        alt=config.pie_menu_alt,\r\n        any=config.pie_menu_any,\r\n        ctrl=config.pie_menu_ctrl,\r\n        oskey=config.pie_menu_oskey,\r\n        shift=config.pie_menu_shift,\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # # point the keymap item to our pie menu\r\n    # kmi.properties.name = \"ATOMIC_MT_main_pie\"\r\n    keymaps.append((km, kmi))",
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 119,

          },

            "lineNumber": 120,

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef add_pie_menu_hotkeys():\r\n    # adds the pie menu hotkeys to blender's addon keymaps\r\n\r\n    global keymaps\r\n    keyconfigs = bpy.context.window_manager.keyconfigs.addon\r\n\r\n    # check to see if a window keymap already exists\r\n    if \"Window\" in keyconfigs.keymaps.keys():\r\n        km = keyconfigs.keymaps['Window']\r\n\r\n    # if not, crate a new one\r\n    else:\r\n        km = keyconfigs.keymaps.new(\r\n            name=\"Window\",\r\n            space_type='EMPTY',\r\n            region_type='WINDOW'\r\n        )\r\n\r\n    # add a new keymap item to that keymap\r\n    kmi = km.keymap_items.new(\r\n        idname=\"atomic.invoke_pie_menu_ui\",\r\n        type=config.pie_menu_type,\r\n        value=\"PRESS\",\r\n        alt=config.pie_menu_alt,\r\n        any=config.pie_menu_any,\r\n        ctrl=config.pie_menu_ctrl,\r\n        oskey=config.pie_menu_oskey,\r\n        shift=config.pie_menu_shift,\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # # point the keymap item to our pie menu\r\n    # kmi.properties.name = \"ATOMIC_MT_main_pie\"\r\n    keymaps.append((km, kmi))",

Here's how to do it:\n\n1. **Open the Command Palette in VS Code**:\n   - Press &#96;Ctrl+Shift+P&#96; (or &#96;Cmd+Shift+P&#96; on macOS) to open the Command Palette.\n\n2. **Access Blender Development Commands**:\n   - Type &#96;Blender: Configure Add-on Development&#96; and select it from the list.\n\n3. **Set the Add-on Module Name**:\n   - In the configuration prompt, specify the module name that matches your add-on's folder name but replaces hyphens with underscores. For example, if your folder is named &#96;atomic-data-manager&#96;, set the module name to &#96;atomic_data_manager&#96;.\n\nBy configuring the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension with the correct module name, VS Code should be able to import your add-on without issues.\n\nFor more detailed information on setting up Blender development in VS Code, you can refer to the [Blender Developer Documentation on Visual Studio Code](https://developer.blender.org/docs/handbook/development_environments/vscode/).   "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "The &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension for Blender development, created by Jacques Lucke, doesn't require specific configuration files like &#96;defaultsettings.json&#96; or &#96;@settings.json&#96;. Instead, it operates based on the structure of your Blender add-on project. To ensure proper functionality, your add-on should be organized with a main directory containing an &#96;__init__.py&#96; file. This setup allows the extension to create a symbolic link (symlink) from your development directory to Blender's add-ons directory, facilitating seamless development and testing. ([github.com](https://github.com/JacquesLucke/blender_vscode?utm_source=openai))\n\nIf you're encountering issues related to module names with hyphens, it's advisable to rename your add-on directory to use underscores or remove the hyphens. This change aligns with Python's module naming conventions and should resolve import errors.\n\nFor more detailed guidance on setting up your development environment with Visual Studio Code for Blender add-on development, you can refer to the Blender Developer Documentation.         "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the Atomic preferences UI, preferences properties, and\r\nsome functions for syncing the preference properties with external factors.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\n# updater removed in Blender 4.5 extension format\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _get_addon_prefs():\r\n    # robustly find our AddonPreferences instance regardless of module name\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"bl_idname\") and ap.bl_idname == ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel.bl_idname:\r\n            return ap\r\n        # fallback: match by known property\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains the Atomic preferences UI, preferences properties, and\r\nsome functions for syncing the preference properties with external factors.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nfrom bpy.utils import register_class\r\nfrom bpy.utils import unregister_class\r\nfrom .. import config\r\n# updater removed in Blender 4.5 extension format\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _get_addon_prefs():\r\n    # robustly find our AddonPreferences instance regardless of module name\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"bl_idname\") and ap.bl_idname == ATOMIC_PT_preferences_panel.bl_idname:\r\n            return ap\r\n        # fallback: match by known property\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file handles the registration of the atomic_data_manager.ui package\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nfrom . import main_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import stats_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import inspect_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import pie_menu_ui\r\nfrom . import preferences_ui\r\nfrom . import support_me_ui\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    # register preferences first so we can access variables in config.py\r\n    preferences_ui.register()\r\n\r\n    # register everything else\r\n    main_panel_ui.register()\r\n    stats_panel_ui.register()\r\n    inspect_ui.register()\r\n    missing_file_ui.register()\r\n    pie_menu_ui.register()\r\n    support_me_ui.register()",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file handles the registration of the atomic_data_manager.ui package\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nfrom . import main_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import stats_panel_ui\r\nfrom . import inspect_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import missing_file_ui\r\nfrom . import pie_menu_ui\r\nfrom . import preferences_ui\r\nfrom . import support_me_ui\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register():\r\n    # register preferences first so we can access variables in config.py\r\n    preferences_ui.register()\r\n\r\n    # register everything else\r\n    main_panel_ui.register()\r\n    stats_panel_ui.register()\r\n    inspect_ui.register()\r\n    missing_file_ui.register()\r\n    pie_menu_ui.register()\r\n    support_me_ui.register()",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions that detect missing files in the Blender\r\nproject.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nimport os\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_missing(data):\r\n    # returns a list of keys of unpacked data-blocks with non-existent\r\n    # filepaths\r\n\r\n    missing = []\r\n\r\n    # list of keys that should not be flagged\r\n    do_not_flag = [\"Render Result\", \"Viewer Node\", \"D-NOISE Export\"]\r\n\r\n    for datablock in data:\r\n\r\n        # the absolute path to our data-block\r\n        abspath = bpy.path.abspath(datablock.filepath)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is not packed and has an invalid filepath\r\n        if not datablock.packed_files and not os.path.isfile(abspath):\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is packed but it does not have a filepath\r\n        elif datablock.packed_files and not abspath:\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n    return missing",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "\"\"\"\r"

            "text": "it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "License, or (at your option) any later version.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "\r"

            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "Atomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r"

            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "---\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "This file contains functions that detect missing files in the Blender\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "project.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "\r"

            "lineNumber": 24,

            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "        elif datablock.packed_files and not abspath:\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 54,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 56,

            "lineNumber": 57,

            "lineNumber": 58,

            "lineNumber": 59,

            "isSignature": true,

            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "def images():\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    # returns a list of keys of images with a non-existent filepath\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\r\nGeneral Public License for more details.\r\n\r\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\r\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains functions that detect missing files in the Blender\r\nproject.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport bpy\r\nimport os\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_missing(data):\r\n    # returns a list of keys of unpacked data-blocks with non-existent\r\n    # filepaths\r\n\r\n    missing = []\r\n\r\n    # list of keys that should not be flagged\r\n    do_not_flag = [\"Render Result\", \"Viewer Node\", \"D-NOISE Export\"]\r\n\r\n    for datablock in data:\r\n\r\n        # the absolute path to our data-block\r\n        abspath = bpy.path.abspath(datablock.filepath)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is not packed and has an invalid filepath\r\n        if not datablock.packed_files and not os.path.isfile(abspath):\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n        # if data-block is packed but it does not have a filepath\r\n        elif datablock.packed_files and not abspath:\r\n\r\n            # if data-block is not in our do not flag list\r\n            # append it to the missing data list\r\n            if datablock.name not in do_not_flag:\r\n                missing.append(datablock.name)\r\n\r\n    return missing",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 4,
            "line": 64
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "stats/missing.py"
      },
      "score": 0.15104269981384277
    },

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_addon_prefs():\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Get the addon preferences instance, compatible across versions.\r\n    \r\n    Returns:\r\n        AddonPreferences or None: The addon preferences instance if found\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_addon_prefs():\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Get the addon preferences instance, compatible across versions.\r\n    \r\n    Returns:\r\n        AddonPreferences or None: The addon preferences instance if found\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    prefs = bpy.context.preferences\r\n    for addon in prefs.addons.values():\r\n        ap = getattr(addon, \"preferences\", None)\r\n        if ap and hasattr(ap, \"enable_missing_file_warning\"):\r\n            return ap\r\n    return None",

        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\nThis file contains global copies of Atomic's preferences so that they\r\ncan be easily access throughout the add-on.\r\n\r\nNOTE:\r\nChanging the values of these variables will NOT change the values in the\r\nAtomic's preferences.         "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\r\n\r\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\r\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\r\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\r\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\r\n\r\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\r\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\r\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. If you want to change a setting, change it in\r\nBlender, not in here.\r\n\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\n# visible atomic preferences\r\nenable_missing_file_warning = True\r\nenable_support_me_popup = True\r\ninclude_fake_users = False\r\nenable_pie_menu_ui = True\r\n\r\n# hidden atomic preferences\r\npie_menu_type = \"D\"\r\npie_menu_alt = False\r\npie_menu_any = False\r\npie_menu_ctrl = False\r\npie_menu_oskey = False\r\npie_menu_shift = False\r\nlast_popup_day = 0",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\"\"\"\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\n\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\nGeneral Public License for more details.\n\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\n\n---\n\nThis module provides version detection and comparison utilities for\nmulti-version Blender support (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0).\n\n\"\"\"\n\nimport bpy\n\n# Version constants\nVERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0)\nVERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0)\nVERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)\n\n\ndef get_blender_version():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the current Blender version as a tuple (major, minor, patch).\n    \n    Returns:\n        tuple: (major, minor, patch) version numbers\n    \"\"\"\n    return bpy.app.version",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,

          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "This module provides version detection and comparison utilities for"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "multi-version Blender support (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "\"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "import bpy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "# Version constants"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "VERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "VERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "VERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "def get_blender_version():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "    Returns the current Blender version as a tuple (major, minor, patch)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "    Returns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "        tuple: (major, minor, patch) version numbers"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "    return bpy.app.version"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "def get_version_string():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "    Returns the current Blender version as a string (e.g., \"4.2.0\")."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "    "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "    Returns:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "        str: Version string in format \"major.minor.patch\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\nCopyright (C) 2019 Remington Creative\n\nThis file is part of Atomic Data Manager.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is free software: you can redistribute\nit and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License\nas published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the\nLicense, or (at your option) any later version.\n\nAtomic Data Manager is distributed in the hope that it will\nbe useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty\nof MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU\nGeneral Public License for more details.\n\nYou should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along\nwith Atomic Data Manager.  If not, see \u003chttps://www.gnu.org/licenses/\u003e.\n\n---\n\nThis module provides version detection and comparison utilities for\nmulti-version Blender support (4.2 LTS, 4.5 LTS, and 5.0).\n\n\"\"\"\n\nimport bpy\n\n# Version constants\nVERSION_4_2_LTS = (4, 2, 0)\nVERSION_4_5_LTS = (4, 5, 0)\nVERSION_5_0 = (5, 0, 0)\n\n\ndef get_blender_version():\n    \"\"\"\n    Returns the current Blender version as a tuple (major, minor, patch).\n    \n    Returns:\n        tuple: (major, minor, patch) version numbers\n    \"\"\"\n    return bpy.app.version",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 51
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "utils/version.py"
      },

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n\r\ndef objects():\r\n    # returns the keys of all unnamed materials in the project\r\n    # NOTE: lists of default names must be tuples!\r\n\r\n    # the default names all curve objects\r\n    curve_names = (\r\n        \"BezierCircle\",\r\n        \"BezierCurve\",\r\n        \"NurbsCircle\",\r\n        \"NurbsCurve\",\r\n        \"NurbsPath\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # the default names of all grease pencil objects\r\n    gpencil_names = (\r\n        \"GPencil\",\r\n        \"Stroke\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # the default names of all light objects\r\n    light_names = (\r\n        \"Area\",\r\n        \"Light\",\r\n        \"Point\",\r\n        \"Spot\",\r\n        \"Sun\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # the default names of all light probe objects\r\n    lprobe_names = (\r\n        \"IrradianceVolume\",\r\n        \"ReflectionCubemap\",\r\n        \"ReflectionPlane\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # the default names of all mesh objects\r\n    mesh_names = (\r\n        \"Circle\",\r\n        \"Cone\",\r\n        \"Cube\",\r\n        \"Cylinder\",\r\n        \"Grid\",\r\n        \"Icosphere\",\r\n        \"Plane\",\r\n        \"Sphere\",\r\n        \"Torus\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # the default names of all miscellaneous objects\r\n    misc_names = (\r\n        \"Mball\",\r\n        \"Text\",\r\n        \"Armature\",\r\n        \"Lattice\",\r\n        \"Empty\",\r\n        \"Camera\",\r\n        \"Speaker\",\r\n        \"Field\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # the default names of all nurbs objects\r\n    nurbs_names = (\r\n        \"SurfCircle\",\r\n        \"SurfCurve\",\r\n        \"SurfPatch\",\r\n        \"SurfTorus\",\r\n        \"Surface\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # the default names of all objects compiled into one tuple\r\n    default_obj_names = \\\r\n        curve_names + gpencil_names + light_names + lprobe_names + \\\r\n        mesh_names + misc_names + nurbs_names\r\n\r\n    unnamed = []\r\n\r\n    for obj in bpy.data.objects:\r\n        if re.match(r'.*\\.\\d\\d\\d$', obj.name) or \\\r\n                obj.name.startswith(default_obj_names):\r\n            unnamed.append(obj.name)\r\n\r\n    return unnamed",

the default names of all objects compiled into one tuple\r\n    default_obj_names = \\\r\n        curve_names + gpencil_names + light_names + lprobe_names + \\\r\n        mesh_names + misc_names + nurbs_names\r\n\r\n    unnamed = []\r\n\r\n    for obj in bpy.data.objects:\r\n        if re.match(r'.*\\.\\d\\d\\d$', obj.name) or \\\r\n                obj.name.startswith(default_obj_names):\r\n            unnamed.append(obj.name)\r\n\r\n    return unnamed",

_**Agent (default)**_


The `vscode_development` addon is configured in Blender's addon preferences, not in VS Code/Cursor settings.

Configure it in Blender:

1. Open Blender
2. Go to Edit → Preferences → Add-ons
3. Find "Development: Visual Studio Code" (or "vscode_development")
4. Expand it and open its preferences
5. Look for a setting to specify the module name or addon path mapping

The addon creates symlinks from your development folder to Blender's addons directory. It's using the folder name `atomic-data-manager` as the module name, which Python can't import.

Options:
- Configure the addon in Blender to use `atomic_data_manager` as the module name
- Rename your development folder from `atomic-data-manager` to `atomic_data_manager` (simplest)

If the addon doesn't have a module name override, renaming the folder is the most reliable fix. The folder name must match the Python module name (underscores, not hyphens).

Should I check if there's a configuration file the addon reads, or do you want to rename the folder?

Added the `blender.addonModules` configuration to your settings.json. This maps your folder path (`e:\atomic-data-manager`) to the correct module name (`atomic_data_manager`).

The configuration tells the VS Code Blender extension to:
- Use `atomic_data_manager` as the Python module name (with underscores)
- Even though your folder is named `atomic-data-manager` (with hyphens)

After saving, restart Blender or reload the extension. The addon should load correctly because the extension will use `atomic_data_manager` as the module name instead of deriving it from the folder name.

If it still doesn't work, try:
1. Restarting Cursor/VS Code
2. Restarting Blender
3. Reloading the addon in Blender (if it's already loaded)

      "chunk": "The &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension for Blender, developed by Jacques Lucke, allows for efficient addon development within Visual Studio Code. To integrate your existing addon with this extension, it's essential to ensure that your addon's folder structure aligns with Blender's expectations.\n\n**Folder Structure Requirements:**\n\nBlender recognizes addons that are organized in a specific directory structure. Your addon should reside within a folder named &#96;addons&#96; inside your designated scripts directory. The typical structure is as follows:\n\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nBlender Stuff\n└── addons\n    └── your-addon-folder\n        ├── __init__.py\n        ├── other_files.py\n        └── ...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\nEnsure that your addon's main script is named &#96;__init__.py&#96; and is placed directly inside its folder. This setup allows Blender to recognize and load your addon correctly. ([github.com](https://github.com/AlansCodeLog/blender-debugger-for-vscode?utm_source=openai))\n\n**Configuring the &#96;vscode_development&#96; Extension:**\n\nOnce your addon's folder structure is correctly set up, you can configure the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension in Visual Studio Code:\n\n1. **Open the Command Palette:** Press &#96;Ctrl+Shift+P&#96; in VS Code.\n\n2. **Start Blender:** Type and select &#96;Blender: Start&#96;. This command will prompt you to select the Blender executable.\n\n3. **Set Up the Addon:** If your addon is already structured correctly, the extension should automatically detect and link it. If not, ensure that your workspace in VS Code is set to the root directory of your addon.\n\nBy following these steps, you should be able to integrate your addon with the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension seamlessly. If you encounter further issues, consider consulting the extension's [GitHub repository](https://github.com/JacquesLucke/blender_vscode) for more detailed documentation and support. ",

      "chunk": "To resolve the issue with the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension not recognizing your addon's folder due to hyphens in its name, you can configure the extension to map the folder name to a valid Python module name. This is done by adding a specific setting in your VS Code &#96;settings.json&#96; file.\n\n**Steps to Configure the &#96;vscode_development&#96; Extension:**\n\n1. **Open VS Code Settings:**\n   - Navigate to &#96;File&#96; \u003e &#96;Preferences&#96; \u003e &#96;Settings&#96; (or press &#96;Ctrl + ,&#96;).\n\n2. **Modify &#96;settings.json&#96;:**\n   - Click on the &#96;{}&#96; icon in the top-right corner to open the &#96;settings.json&#96; file.\n\n3. **Add the Configuration:**\n   - Insert the following configuration, replacing &#96;\"atomic-data-manager\"&#96; with your addon's folder name and &#96;\"atomic_data_manager\"&#96; with the desired module name:\n\n     &#96;&#96;&#96;json\n     {\n       \"blender.addonModules\": {\n         \"atomic-data-manager\": \"atomic_data_manager\"\n       }\n     }\n     &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n   - Ensure that the folder name matches exactly, including case sensitivity.\n\n4. **Save and Restart:**\n   - Save the &#96;settings.json&#96; file.\n   - Restart VS Code to apply the changes.\n\nThis configuration maps the folder name &#96;atomic-data-manager&#96; to the module name &#96;atomic_data_manager&#96;, allowing the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension to correctly import your addon.\n\n**Additional Considerations:**\n\n- **Folder Naming Conventions:**\n  - It's advisable to use underscores (&#96;_&#96;) instead of hyphens (&#96;-&#96;) in folder names for Python modules, as hyphens are not valid in module names.\n\n- **Extension Documentation:**\n  - For more detailed information and advanced configurations, refer to the official documentation of the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension by Jacques Lucke. ([github.com](https://github.com/JacquesLucke/blender_vscode?utm_source=openai))\n\nBy following these steps, your addon should be recognized and function correctly within the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension. This is done by adding a specific setting in your VS Code &#96;settings.json&#96; file.\n\n**Steps to Configure:**\n\n1. **Open VS Code Settings:**\n   - Navigate to &#96;File&#96; \u003e &#96;Preferences&#96; \u003e &#96;Settings&#96; (or press &#96;Ctrl + ,&#96;).\n\n2. **Modify &#96;settings.json&#96;:**\n   - Click on the &#96;{}&#96; icon in the top-right corner to open the &#96;settings.json&#96; file.\n\n3. **Add the Configuration:**\n   - Insert the following configuration, replacing &#96;\"atomic-data-manager\"&#96; with your addon's folder name and &#96;\"atomic_data_manager\"&#96; with the desired module name:\n\n     &#96;&#96;&#96;json\n     {\n       \"blender.addonModules\": {\n         \"atomic-data-manager\": \"atomic_data_manager\"\n       }\n     }\n     &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n   This setting maps the folder name to a valid Python module name by replacing hyphens with underscores.\n\n4. **Save and Restart:**\n   - Save the &#96;settings.json&#96; file.\n   - Restart VS Code to apply the changes.\n\n**Additional Considerations:**\n\n- **Workspace vs. User Settings:**\n  - If you prefer this configuration to apply only to the current project, add it to the &#96;.vscode/settings.json&#96; file within your project directory.\n  - For a global setting across all projects, add it to the user &#96;settings.json&#96; as described above.\n\n- **Verify the Configuration:**\n  - After restarting VS Code, ensure that the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension recognizes the addon correctly.\n  - If issues persist, double-check the folder and module names for accuracy.\n\nBy configuring the &#96;blender.addonModules&#96; setting, you inform the &#96;vscode_development&#96; extension how to interpret your addon's folder structure, allowing it to handle folder names with hyphens appropriately. The `vscode_development` addon inside Blender uses the folder name as the module name, and the VS Code setting isn't being applied. Options:

**Option 1: Rename the folder** (most reliable)
- Rename `e:\atomic-data-manager` to `e:\atomic_data_manager`
- This matches the module name in your `blender_manifest.toml`
- The addon should load correctly

**Option 2: Check if there's a workspace-specific configuration**
- The extension might need workspace settings instead of user settings
- Create `.vscode/settings.json` in your project folder

**Option 3: Configure the vscode_development addon inside Blender**
- The addon running inside Blender might have its own preferences
- Check Blender's addon preferences for `vscode_development`

Given the extension may not be actively maintained, renaming the folder is the most reliable fix. Should I rename it, or do you want to try workspace settings first?
